headline: Agent harness and tooling techniques dominated the quarter while a cluster of new rendering and math primitives broke in.
body: The quarter's rotation concentrated in AI-assisted development tooling, with tool calling leading on ratio and multi-session workflows, scoped skills, LSP, the PIV Loop, cache hit rate, and encrypted reasoning all moving together. Meta-style evaluation harnesses became a recurring theme, with Latent Space framing a shift toward Meta-based testing harnesses and Omnigent launching as an open-source meta harness for agents. Agent design also surfaced new entrants in the doer-judge framework, role confusion, primitive-action decomposition, and emergent behavior, while a separate breakout cluster covered triangle splatting, neural rendering, the Discrete Hartley Transform, and Green's functions. On the geopolitical side Anthropic accused Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities, Europe pushed back on U.S. chip export controls, and Cerebras stock fell after its first public earnings.

cited_items.title: Omnigent: Open-source meta harness for agents
Anthropic accuses Alibaba of illicitly extracting AI capabilities - BBC
Europe is pushing back on Washington’s chip war
Cerebras stock plunges after earnings as CEO says margin outlook was misunderstood
